summary:
The provided literature indicates no direct study evaluating CPB temperature’s effect specifically on postoperative euthyroid sick syndrome; however, it suggests a plausible indirect link through altered inflammation and central nervous system function—both implicated in the pathophysiology of this condition. Therefore, while current data do not establish causality or efficacy, changes in CPB temperature may influence underlying mechanisms contributing to euthyroid sick syndrome via modulation of systemic inflammation and neural control over hormone secretion.

Only summarize the agent's actual reasoning—not your own interpretation.

The agent notes there is no direct evidence linking cardiopulmonary bypass temperature to postoperative euthyroid sick syndrome, though indirectly, both hypothermia and hyperthermia can modulate systemic inflammation and neurological functions involved in its development. Thus, while temperature effects are not proven, they may contribute mechanistically by influencing inflammatory pathways and hypothalamic-thyroid interactions.
